questions.prompt: What is the core idea of equivalence partitioning?
options: Test every possible input value to be thorough
Split inputs into classes treated the same, then test one representative per class
Always test the boundaries of every input
Test only the values users are most likely to enter
questions.explanation: EP groups inputs into behavioural classes and tests one representative of each — coverage without testing every value.

questions.prompt: A quantity field accepts 1–10. Which set is the cleanest equivalence-partition representative set?
options: {0, 5, 15} — one too-low, one valid, one too-high
questions.explanation: Three behavioural classes — too low, valid, too high — so one representative each: 0, 5, 15. (The edges 1 and 10 are BVA's job.)

questions.prompt: Why test only one value from a partition instead of several?
options: Extra values from the same class add cost but little new coverage
Testing more than one value is not allowed
The first value is always the only correct one
It makes the test report shorter
questions.explanation: If the code handles one member of a class correctly it handles the rest the same way, so additional same-class values rarely reveal new defects.

questions.prompt: How do equivalence partitioning and boundary value analysis fit together?
options: They are the same technique with different names
BVA finds the classes; EP tests the edges between them
EP finds the classes; BVA tests the edges between them
They cannot be used on the same field
questions.explanation: EP identifies the partitions first; BVA then tests the boundaries where those partitions meet.

questions.prompt: An email field should reject the whole 'invalid' class. A validator accepts `user@@domain..com`. What does this reveal?
options: Nothing — that address is actually valid
A member of the invalid partition is wrongly accepted, so the partition isn't enforced
The valid partition is too small
The field needs boundary value analysis instead
questions.explanation: `user@@domain..com` belongs to the invalid class. Accepting it means the invalid partition isn't truly enforced — exactly the kind of gap EP is designed to expose.
